target.name: Xsight Labs
target.founder: Guy Koren, Erez Shaizaf, Gal Malach

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $281M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $529M - $7.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: SambaNova Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series D), post-money $5B

private.name: Tenstorrent
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series D), post-money $2.7B

data.narrative:
Xsight Labs, a Series D chip design company, is evaluated against a peer universe of 8 private and 3 public semiconductor firms. The applicable comparables include same-stage peers SambaNova Systems ($5.0B) and Tenstorrent ($2.7B), plus adjacent-stage companies PsiQuantum ($7.0B Series E) and DEEPX ($529M Series C), establishing a $529M–$7.0B reference range.

Two valuation methods triangulate the range. Stage-progression applies a 1.5x step-up multiplier to Xsight's $281M total raised, implying $422M. Peer-median analysis derives $3.9B from the four-company cohort. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied; the public semiconductor median P/S of 24.4x across ALAB, NVDA, and AMD lacks a revenue input for the private target.

The two applicable methods imply a valuation range of $358M (low) to $4.4B (high), with a median of $2.1B. Stage-progression yields the conservative bound, reflecting capital deployment to date. Peer-median anchors to recent Series D and adjacent fundings in the same sector. Key uncertainties include revenue scale and path to profitability relative to comparables, neither disclosed in available data. The wide dispersion ($529M–$7.0B among peers) reflects heterogeneity in chip-sector company maturity and market positions.
